skill.name: agent-md-refactor
skill.description: Refactor bloated AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, and similar agent instruction files into concise root files with linked topic guides.
skill.summary: Organize large agent instruction files using progressive disclosure and focused linked documentation.
skill.author: softaworks

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, crypto, reconnaissance, and URL patterns. AI review found the command, crypto, and reconnaissance matches are Markdown prose, examples, filenames, and tables, not executable behavior. No prompt injection attempt or malicious intent was found; the only URL is an attribution link.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as External Commands
low_findings.description: The static command findings are false positives. The cited lines contain Markdown inline code, fenced examples, file names, and templates, with no script execution path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed context confirms these are documentation examples and Markdown formatting. No shell, Ruby, or executable wrapper is present in the scanned files.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Matches Are Documentation Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings are false positives. The cited lines refer to Markdown files, ordinary table content, or documentation examples, not cryptographic algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding content contains project documentation and template text only. I found no hashing, encryption, signing, or credential handling code.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Matches Are Non-Executable Prose
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance findings are false positives. The cited lines discuss verification, anti-patterns, and example labels, not host or user enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are part of README and SKILL documentation tables or examples. There is no command invocation or data collection behavior.

low_findings.title: External Attribution Link Present
low_findings.description: README.md includes one external URL used as attribution. It is not fetched by the skill and does not transmit local data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears in prose at the top of README.md. I found no network call, download instruction, webhook, or exfiltration path.

content.value_statement: Large agent instruction files waste context and bury important rules. This skill reorganizes them into a small root file and focused linked guides.
actual_capabilities: Finds contradictory instructions before restructuring begins.
Separates universal root instructions from topic-specific guidance.
Groups remaining content into clear files such as testing, architecture, and code style.
Produces a linked root file template for AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, or similar files.
Flags redundant, vague, outdated, or obvious instructions for deletion.
Provides a verification checklist for links, coverage, and root file size.
limitations: It cannot decide project policy conflicts without user input.
It does not validate generated links unless the agent checks the workspace.
It depends on the quality and completeness of the original instruction file.
It is focused on Markdown-style agent instructions, not general documentation sites.

use_cases.title: Clean Up a Growing CLAUDE.md
use_cases.description: Turn a long root file into a concise overview with linked topic guides.

use_cases.title: Standardize Repository Agent Instructions
use_cases.description: Create a repeatable structure for AGENTS.md across multiple codebases.

use_cases.title: Prepare Onboarding Guidance for AI Tools
use_cases.description: Separate setup, testing, architecture, and style rules so assistants can find relevant guidance quickly.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Cleanup
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one bloated root file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor my CLAUDE.md using progressive disclosure. Keep only universal instructions in the root file and move the rest into linked topic files.

prompt_templates.title: Resolve Conflicts First
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request when instructions may conflict.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze my AGENTS.md for contradictions first. Ask me to resolve conflicts, then propose a cleaner linked file structure.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-File Instruction Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for scattered project guidance.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review all agent instruction files in this repository. Consolidate duplicated guidance, preserve project-specific rules, and create a minimal root file with links.

prompt_templates.title: Strict Refactor With Verification
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a complete migration and quality check.
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor the agent instructions into 3 to 8 focused files. Flag deletions separately, verify every link, and confirm no actionable instruction was lost.

output_examples.input: A 300-line CLAUDE.md mixes build commands, TypeScript style, test rules, and deployment notes.
output: A short root file with project summary, essential commands, and links.
Separate topic files for testing, TypeScript, code style, deployment, and architecture.
A deletion report for vague or redundant instructions.

output_examples.input: An AGENTS.md says both to use semicolons and to avoid semicolons.
output_examples.output: A contradiction report asks which style rule should win before the refactor continues.

output_examples.input: A repository has scattered AI assistant notes in several Markdown files.
output_examples.output: A consolidated structure keeps universal guidance in the root file and moves specialized rules into linked guides.

best_practices: Start from a clean git state so every documentation change is easy to review.
Resolve contradictions before moving content into new files.
Keep the root file focused on rules needed for every task.
anti_patterns: Do not keep detailed language, testing, and architecture rules in the root file.
Do not split content into so many files that navigation becomes harder.
Do not delete project-specific rules unless they are clearly redundant or obsolete.

faq.question: Which files does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, COPILOT.md, and similar Markdown instruction files for AI coding assistants.

faq.question: Does it automatically resolve contradictions?
faq.answer: No. It identifies contradictions and asks the user which instruction should take precedence.

faq.question: Will it preserve every instruction?
faq.answer: It aims to preserve actionable instructions and separately flags vague, redundant, outdated, or obvious text for deletion.

faq.question: How small should the root file be?
faq.answer: The skill targets a root file under about 50 lines with only universal project guidance.

faq.question: Can it work with Codex and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill supports Claude, Codex, and Claude Code style instruction files.

faq.question: Does it require running scripts?
faq.answer: No. The skill is documentation guidance and templates. It does not include executable scripts.
